steps.title: Spot the defining claim
steps.content: The researchers' conclusion is that net CO<sub>2</sub> is likely to INCREASE when warming hastens spring snow melt. This reveals what must be true: the mechanism of early snow melt must favor CO<sub>2</sub> output over absorption.

steps.title: Apply the conclusion to the choices
steps.content:
Early snow melt reduces the amount of soil moisture available for the growth of plant species such as *C. palustre*, and it raises the rate of heterotrophic respiration. describes early snow melt reducing soil moisture (cutting plant CO2 absorption) and raising heterotrophic respiration (increasing soil CO2 output)—this two-pronged effect directly produces the predicted net increase in CO2.

The other choices either increase absorption without affecting output, weaken both processes, or leave the net outcome unclear.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
